text:
Once upon a time, there was a young man named Ben who loved collecting stamps. Every morning after school, before heading to his part-time job at the post office, Ben would spend a few blissful minutes flipping through his vast collection of rare and exotic stamps.

In his room, adorned with shelves stocked full of albums and drawers filled with neatly organized specimens, Ben's stamp collection was a testament to his passion. He had traveled the world to acquire pieces, acquiring stamps from every corner of the globe. It mattered not just the rarity or the beauty; each one held a story, a piece of history that Ben sought to understand.

One fateful autumn afternoon, as Ben was carefully selecting a new addition to his collection, his favorite album fell victim to a mishap. The stamp he had been obsessing over was now smudged and displaced. Disheartened, he shut his album, feeling a pang of depression because without his prized stamp, the overall beauty of his collection seemed diminished.
